				\draw[line width=0pt,color=white,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.10] (0,0)--(0,4) --(5,4)-- (5,0)--(0,0);
				\draw [rotate around={0.:(0.,0.)},line width=0.5pt,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.10] (0,0) circle (2.2361);
				\draw[line width=0.5pt,color=red](-1,2.5)-- (5,-0.5);
				\draw[line width=0.5pt,color=yellow](0,4)-- (0,-3);
				\draw[line width=0.5pt,color=yellow](-3,0)-- (3,0);
				\draw[line width=2pt,color=yellow](0,2) -- (0,0)-- (2.2361,0);
				\draw[line width=2pt,color=red](0,2)-- (2,1);
				\draw[line width=2pt,color=blue] (2.2361,0) arc (0:27:2.2361);
				%\draw[line width=2pt,color=blue] (0,2.2361) arc (90:63:2.2361);
				\begin{scriptsize}
					%\draw [fill=black] (1,2) circle (2pt) node[above right] {\footnotesize $(1,2)$};
					\draw [fill=black] (2,1) circle (2pt) node[above right] {\footnotesize $(2,1)$};
					\draw [fill=black] (0,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {\footnotesize $(0,0)$};
					\draw [fill=black] (0,2) circle (2pt) node[above] {\footnotesize $(0,2)$};
					\draw [fill=black] (2.2361,0) circle (2pt) node[below right] {\footnotesize $(\sqrt{5},0)$};
					\draw [fill=orange,orange] (3,2) circle (2pt) node[below] {\tiny $(3,2)$};
					
				\end{scriptsize}
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
	\end{columns}
\end{frame}


\subsection{Condiciones de Karush-Kuhn-Tucker}
%\end{document}
%%
%%
\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT}\small
	\begin{itemize}\itemsep 1mm
		\item Hay problemas en los que  hay puntos F-J para los que con $u_0=0$.
		\item Por ejemplo, cuando $\G_0=\emptyset$, ya que las condiciones se verifican independientemente de la funci\'{o}n objetivo. 
		\item Karush en 1939, e independientemente Kuhn y Tucker en 1951 desarrollaron condiciones  que imponen nuevas hip\'{o}tesis que garantizan que $u_0\neq 0$
		\item Se logra que la funci\'{o}n objetivo intervenga en las condiciones de optimalidad. 
		\item En concreto, se añade la condición de independencia de los gradientes. 
		\item Estas condiciones no se aplican a todos los puntos, sino que se excluyen algunos; por tanto, NO son suficientes y puede que el \'{o}ptimo global del problema est\'{e} entre los puntos excluidos (por ejemplo, si  en este punto los gradientes de las funciones activas son linealmente dependientes).
		\item Para problemas lineales, sí resultan ser condiciones necesarias y suficientes.
	\end{itemize}
%%
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT}\small
\begin{teorema}[Condiciones necesarias de Karush-Kuhn-Tucker]
Sea el problema $\min\{f(\vx)\,/\, \vx\in\X,\;\; g_i(\vx)\leq 0,\; i=1,\dots,m\}$,  $\ovx$ una soluci\'{o}n factible, e $\I=\{i\,/\, g_i(\ovx)=0\}$ tal que 1) $f$ $g_i$, $i\in\I$ son diferenciables en $\ovx$,  2) $g_i$, $i\notin\I$ son continuas en $\ovx$ y 3) $\gg_i(\ovx)$, $i\in\I$, son linealmente independientes.

\smallskip

Entonces, si $\ovx$ es un m\'{\i}nimo local de $f$,  existen escalares $\{u_i, i\in\I\}$, con $u_i\geq 0$, $\forall i\in\I$ tales que:
	$$
	\gf(\ovx)+\sum_{i\in\I} u_i\gg_i(\ovx)=\vv.
	$$
	\\
	Si adem\'{a}s se verifica que las funciones $\{g_i,\, i\notin \I\}$ son diferenciables en $\ovx$,
	las condiciones anteriores se pueden escribir de la forma
	\begin{align*}
	\gf(\ovx)+\sum_{i=1}^m u_i\gg_i(\ovx)  =\vv, &
	\\
	u_ig_i(\ovx) =0, & \qquad\forall i=1,\dots,m,
	\\
	u_i\geq 0, & \qquad\forall i=1,\dots,m.
	\end{align*}
\end{teorema}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT}
\alert{Demostración. }
\begin{itemize}\itemsep 2mm
	\item 	De las condiciones necesarias de Fritz John, existen escalares $u_0$ y 	$\hat{u}_i$, $i\in\I$, no negativos y no todos iguales a 0, tales que:
	$$
	u_0\gf(\ovx)+\sum_{i\in\I}\hat{u}_i\gg_i(\ovx)=0.
	$$
	\item Por ser $\gg_i(\ovx)$, $i\in\I$ linealmente independientes, se tiene que $u_0 > 0$. 
	\item Si no, el vector $\vv$ se podría poner como combinación lineal de existir\'{\i}a una combinaci\'{o}n lineal de $\gg_i(\ovx)$, $i\in\I$, con alguna coordenada  no nula, lo que es contradictorio.
	\item 
	Tomando $u_i=\frac{\hat{u}_i}{u_o}$, $\forall i\in\I$, se obtiene la primera parte de la demostraci\'{o}n.
	\item Tomando, adem\'{a}s, $u_i=0$, $\forall i\notin \I$, se obtiene la segunda.
\end{itemize}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT}

Como en el caso de las condiciones de Fritz John:
\begin{itemize}
	\item Los escalares $u_i$ se denominan
	multiplicadores de Lagrange.
	\item  Las condiciones que implican que $\ovx$ sean factibles se
	denominan condiciones de {\em factibilidad primal}, 
	\item La condici\'{o}n de que
	$$
	\gf(\ovx)+\sum_{i=1}^m u_i\gg_i(\ovx)  =0,
	$$
	se denomina condici\'{o}n de {\em factibilidad dual}.
	\item Las condiciones de que
	$u_ig_i(\vx)=0$, $\forall i=1,\dots,m$, se denominan condiciones de {\em holgura
		complementaria}.
\end{itemize}
Las tres condiciones (factibilidad primal y dual y de holgura complementaria)  se denominan condiciones de {\em Karush-Kuhn-Tucker, KKT}, y los puntos que
las verifican son puntos KKT.
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT}
\begin{observacion}
		Si el punto $\ovx$ es un m\'{a}ximo local, entonces las condiciones siguen siendo v\'{a}lidas sin m\'{a}s
		que pedir que los multiplicadores sean no positivos.
\end{observacion}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}[t]{Condiciones KKT. Ejemplo}
Plantear las ecuaciones de Karush-Kuhn-Tucker y encontrar los candidatos a m\'{\i}nimo y máximos del siguiente problema:
	\begin{columns}[t]
	\column[]{0.4\textwidth}{\vspace*{-7mm}
		\begin{align*}
			\min\text{ y }\max \, & x_1+x_2
			\\
			& x_1^2+x_2^2-4\leq 0
			\\
			& -x_1^2+x_2\leq 0
			\end{align*}
			\only<2>{
			Gradientes:
			\begin{align*}
				\gf(\vx) & =\binom{1}{1}	\\
				\gg_1(\vx) & =\binom{2x_1}{2x_2}	\\
				\gg_2(\vx) & =\binom{-2x_1}{1}	\\
			\end{align*}
			}
			\only<3>{
			Sistema KKT:\vspace*{-2mm}
			\begin{align*}
			1+2x_1u_1-2x_1u_2 & =0
			\\
			1+2x_2u_1+u_2    & =0
			\\
			u_1(x_1^2+x_2^2-4)& =0
			\\
			u_2(x_1^2-x_2) & =0
			\\
			u_1,u_2\geq 0 &\quad  (\min)
			\\
			u_1,u_2\leq 0 &\quad  (\max)
			\end{align*}
			}
	}
	\column[t]{0.6\textwidth}{
		\begin{center}
	\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=1.2]
	\clip(-2.2,-2.2) rectangle (3.6,2.6);    
	\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-2.2,-2.2) grid (4.2,2.6);
	\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-2.2,0.) -- (4.2,0.);
	\foreach \x in {-2,-1,1,2,3,4} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
	\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0.,-2.2) -- (0.,2.6);
	\foreach \y in {-2,-1,1,2} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
	\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
	\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.25] (-10,-10) -- (-10,10) -- (10,10) -- (10,-10) -- (-10,-10);
	\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2.2:4.2,color=yellow,fill=white,fill opacity=1,samples=100] plot(\x,{(\x*\x)});
	\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.25] (0.,0.) circle (2cm);
	\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(--3.-1.*\x)/1.});
	\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(--3.5-1.*\x)/1.});
	\draw [->,line width=1.5pt, color=red] (2.5,1.) -- +(0.75,0.75) node[above] {$\gf$};
	\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
	}
\end{columns}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT. Ejemplo}
	\begin{itemize}
		\item Si $u_1=0$ , necesariamente tiene que ser $u_2\neq 0$.
		
		El sistema resulta:
		\begin{align*}
		1-2x_1u_2 & =0
		\\
		1+u_2    & =0
		\\
		x_1^2-x_2 & =0
		\end{align*}
		De la segunda ecuación se obtiene $u_2=-1$. 
		
		\smallskip
		
		Entonces, de la primera $x_1=-\frac{1}{2}$ y, por la tercera, $x_2=\frac{1}{4}$ .
		
		\smallskip
		
		Se obtiene el punto  A$=(-\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{4})$, que es un candidato a m\'{a}ximo.
	\end{itemize}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT. Ejemplo}
	\begin{center}
		\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=1.5]
		\clip(-2.2,-2.2) rectangle (3.6,2.6);    
		\only<2->{
				\begin{scope}[scale=3,shift={(0.5,0)}]
				\clip(-1.25,-0.5) rectangle (0.25,1); 
		}
		\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-2.2,-2.2) grid (4.2,2.6);
		\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-2.2,0.) -- (4.2,0.);
		\foreach \x in {-2,-1,1,2,3,4} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
		\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0.,-2.2) -- (0.,2.6);
		\foreach \y in {-2,-1,1,2} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
		\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
		\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.25] (-10,-10) -- (-10,10) -- (10,10) -- (10,-10) -- (-10,-10);
		\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2.2:4.2,color=yellow,fill=white,fill opacity=1,samples=100] plot(\x,{(\x*\x)});
		\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.25] (0.,0.) circle (2cm);
		\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(--3.-\x)});
		\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(--3.5-\x)});
		\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-0.25-\x)});
		\draw [->,line width=1.5pt, color=red] (2.5,1.) -- +(0.75,0.75) node[above] {$\gf$};
		\only<1>{
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-0.5,0.25) -- (0.5,1.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-0.5,0.25) -- (0.3,1.05);
			\draw [fill=black] (-0.5,0.25) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {A};
		}
		\only<2->{
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-0.5,0.25) -- + (0.25,0.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-0.5,0.25) -- +(0.2,0.2);
			\draw [fill=black] (-0.5,0.25) circle (0.5pt) node[below] {A};
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-0.15-\x)});
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-0.05-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-0.35-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-0.45-\x)});
			\end{scope}
		}
		\end{tikzpicture}
	\end{center}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT. Ejemplo}
		\begin{itemize}
			\item Si $u_1\neq 0$ y $u_2=0$, el sistema resulta:
					\begin{align*}
							1+2x_1u_1 & =0
							\\
							1+2x_2u_1    & =0
							\\
							x_1^2+x_2^2-4 & =0
					\end{align*}
				Despejando $x_1$ y $x_2$ en las dos primeras ecuaciones:
				$$
				x_1=\frac{-1}{2u_1}\qquad x_2=\frac{-1}{2u_1}
				$$
				De la tercera ecuaci\'{o}n se obtiene: $u_1=\pm\frac{\sqrt{2}}{4}$.
				\begin{itemize}
							\item $u_1=+\frac{\sqrt{2}}{4}$: punto B=$(x_1=-\sqrt{2},x_2=-\sqrt{2})$. Candidato a mínimo.
							\item $u_1=-\frac{\sqrt{2}}{4}$: punto C=$(x_1=+\sqrt{2}, x_2=+\sqrt{2})$. Candidato a máximo.
				\end{itemize}
		\end{itemize}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT. Ejemplo}
\begin{center}
	\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=1.5]
	\clip(-2.2,-2.2) rectangle (3.6,2.6);    
	\only<2>{
		\begin{scope}[scale=3,shift={(-1,-1)}]
		\clip(0.5,0.5) rectangle (2,2); 
	}
	\only<3>{
			\begin{scope}[scale=3,shift={(1.5,1.5)}]
			\clip(-2,-2) rectangle (-0.5,-0.5); 
	}
	
	\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-2.2,-2.2) grid (4.2,2.6);
	\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-2.2,0.) -- (4.2,0.);
	\foreach \x in {-2,-1,1,2,3,4} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
	\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0.,-2.2) -- (0.,2.6);
	\foreach \y in {-2,-1,1,2} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
	\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
	\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.25] (-10,-10) -- (-10,10) -- (10,10) -- (10,-10) -- (-10,-10);
	\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2.2:4.2,color=yellow,fill=white,fill opacity=1,samples=100] plot(\x,{(\x*\x)});
	\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.25] (0.,0.) circle (2cm);
%	\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.-1.*\x)/1.});
	\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.5-1.*\x)/1.});
	\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-2.828426-\x)});
	\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(2.828426-\x)});
	\draw [->,line width=1.5pt, color=red] (2.5,1.) -- +(0.75,0.75) node[above] {$\gf$};


		\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-0.5,0.25) -- (0.5,1.25);
		%\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-1.25,1.562) -- (-0.25,2.562);
		%\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (1.25,1.562) -- (2.25,2.562);
		%\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (-1.25,1.562) -- (-1.875,2.343);
		%\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (1.25,1.562) -- (1.875,2.343);
		\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-0.5,0.25) -- (0.3,1.05);
		%\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-1.25,1.562) -- (-0.25,1.962);
		%\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (1.25,1.562) -- (0.25,1.962);
		\only<1>{
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) -- (-0.41421356237309515,-0.41421356237309515);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) -- (2.414213562373095,2.414213562373095);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) -- (2.121320343559643,2.121320343559643);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) -- (-2.121320343559643,-2.121320343559643);
			\draw [fill=black] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) circle (1.2pt) node[above] {B};
			\draw [fill=black] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {C};
			\draw [fill=black] (-0.5,0.25) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {A};
		}
		\only<2->{
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) --+ (0.25,0.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) --+ (0.25,0.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) --+ (0.2,0.2);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) --+ (-0.2,-0.2);
			
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-2.7-\x)});
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-2.6-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-3-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-3.1-\x)});
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.0-\x)});
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.1-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(2.7-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(2.6-\x)});
			
			\draw [fill=black] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) circle (.5pt) node[above] {B};;
			\draw [fill=black] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) circle (.5pt) node[below] {C};
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-0.5,0.25) -- + (0.25,0.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-0.5,0.25) -- +(0.2,0.2);
			\draw [fill=black] (-0.5,0.25) circle (0.5pt) node[below] {A};
			\end{scope}
		}
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT. Ejemplo}
\begin{itemize}
		\item Si $u_1\neq 0$ y $u_2\neq 0$, el sistema resulta:
			\begin{align*}
			1+2x_1u_1-2x_1u_2 & =0
			\\
			1+2x_2u_1+u_2    & =0
			\\
			x_1^2+x_2^2-4 & =0
			\\
			-x_1^2+x_2 & =0
			\\
			u_1,u_2\geq 0 &
			\end{align*}
			La soluciones son los puntos intersecci\'{o}n de las restricciones:
			\begin{itemize}
				\item El punto $E$, con  $x_2=\frac{-1+\sqrt{17}}{2}$ y $x_1=+\sqrt{\frac{-1+\sqrt{17}}{2}}$,  resulta
				$$
				u_1\approx -0.339\qquad u_2\approx 0.059.
				$$
				No es un candidato ni a m\'{a}ximo ni a m\'{\i}nimo.
				\item El punto $D$, con $x_2=\frac{-1+\sqrt{17}}{2}$ y $x_1=-\sqrt{\frac{-1+\sqrt{17}}{2}}$, resulta 
				$$
				u_1\approx -0.145 \qquad u_2\approx -0.545.
				$$
				Es un candidato a m\'{a}ximo.
			\end{itemize}
		\end{itemize}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT. Ejemplo}
\begin{columns}
\column{0.28\textwidth}
\visible<4>{
		\begin{align*}
		A\,:\, &\text{ni máx ni mín} \\
		B\,:\, &\text{mín global } \\
		C\,:\, &\text{máx global} \\
		D\,:\, &\text{máx local} \\
		E\,:\, &\text{ni máx ni mín} 
		\end{align*}
}
\column{0.8\textwidth}
\begin{center}
	\scalebox{0.9}{
	\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=1.5]
	\clip(-2.2,-2.2) rectangle (3.6,2.6);    
	\only<2>{
		\begin{scope}[scale=3,shift={(-1,-1.25)}]
		\clip(0.5,0.75) rectangle (2,2.25); 
	}
	\only<3>{
		\begin{scope}[scale=3,shift={(1.5,-1.25)}]
		\clip(-2,0.75) rectangle (-0.5,2.25); 
	}
	\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-2.2,-2.2) grid (4.2,2.6);
	\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-2.2,0.) -- (4.2,0.);
	\foreach \x in {-2,-1,1,2,3,4} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
	\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0.,-2.2) -- (0.,2.6);
	\foreach \y in {-2,-1,1,2} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
	\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
	\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.25] (-10,-10) -- (-10,10) -- (10,10) -- (10,-10) -- (-10,-10);
	\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2.2:4.2,color=yellow,fill=white,fill opacity=1,samples=100] plot(\x,{(\x*\x)});
	\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.25] (0.,0.) circle (2cm);
	%\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.-\x)});
	%\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.5-\x)});
	\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-1.25+1.56-\x)});
	\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(1.25+1.56-\x)});
	%\draw [->,line width=1.5pt, color=red] (2.5,1.) -- +(0.75,0.75) node[above] {$\gf$};
	\only<1,4>{
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (-1.25,1.562) -- (-1.875,2.343);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (1.25,1.562) -- (1.875,2.343);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-0.5,0.25) -- (0.3,1.05);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-1.25,1.562) -- (-0.25,1.962);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (1.25,1.562) -- (0.25,1.962);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-0.5,0.25) -- (0.5,1.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-1.25,1.562) -- (-0.25,2.562);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (1.25,1.562) -- (2.25,2.562);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) -- (-0.41421356237309515,-0.41421356237309515);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) -- (2.414213562373095,2.414213562373095);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) -- (2.121320343559643,2.121320343559643);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) -- (-2.121320343559643,-2.121320343559643);
			\draw [fill=black] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) circle (1.2pt) node[above] {B};;
			\draw [fill=black] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {C};
			\draw [fill=black] (1.25,1.562) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {E};;
			\draw [fill=black] (-1.25,1.562) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {D};;
			\draw [fill=black] (-0.5,0.25) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {A};
	}
	\only<4>{
				\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-0.25-\x)});
				\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-2.828426-\x)});
				\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(2.828426-\x)});
	}
	\only<2-3>{
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (-1.25,1.562) -- + (-0.3125,0.3905);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (1.25,1.562) -- + (0.3125,0.3905);
		
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-1.25,1.562) --+ (0.5,0.2);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (1.25,1.562) --+ (-0.5,0.2);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (1.25,1.562 ) --+ (0.25,0.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-1.25,1.562 ) --+ (0.25,0.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) --+ (0.25,0.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) --+ (0.25,0.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) --+ (0.2,0.2);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) --+ (0.2,0.2);
			
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-1.15+1.56-\x)});
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-1.05+1.56-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-1.35+1.56-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-1.45+1.56-\x)});
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.0-\x)});
			\draw [color=red,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.1-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(2.7-\x)});
			\draw [color=blue,line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(2.6-\x)});
			
			\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-0.5,0.25) -- + (0.25,0.25);
			\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-0.5,0.25) -- +(0.2,0.2);
			\draw [fill=black] (1.25,1.562) circle (0.5pt) node[below] {E};;
			\draw [fill=black] (-1.25,1.562) circle (0.5pt) node[below] {D};;
			\draw [fill=black] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) circle (.5pt) node[below] {C};
			\end{scope}
	}
	\end{tikzpicture}}
\end{center}
\end{columns}
	%%
	%%
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Condiciones suficientes de  KKT}\small
	\begin{teorema}[Condiciones suficientes de Karush-Kuhn-Tucker]\label{kkt-suf-d}
		Sea el problema $\min\{f(\vx)\,/\, \vx\in\X,\;\; g_i(\vx)\leq 0,\; i=1,\dots,m\}$, $\ovx$ un solución factible, e $\I=\{i\,/\, g_i(\ovx)=0\}$ el conjunto de restricciones activas asociadas a $\ovx$. Si
		\begin{enumerate}
			\item $f$ es pseudo-convexa en $\ovx$,
			\item $g_i$, $i\in\I$, son cuasi-convexas y diferenciables en $\ovx$,
			\item $\ovx$ cumple las condiciones necesarias de Karush-Kuhn-Tucker,
		\end{enumerate}
		entonces $\ovx$ es un m\'{\i}nimo global del problema.
	\end{teorema}\footnotesize
	\begin{observacion}
		Si las condiciones de pseudo-convexidad, cuasi-convexidad y diferenciabilidad se verifican
		s\'{o}lo localmente, entonces $\ovx$ ser\'{a} un m\'{\i}nimo local.
		
		\medskip
		
		Si el punto $\ovx$ es un m\'{a}ximo local, entonces, para que sea m\'{a}ximo global en la primera
		condici\'{o}n hay que exigir que $f$ sea pseudo-c\'{o}ncava ($-f$ pseudo-convexa).
	\end{observacion}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}[allowframebreaks]{Condiciones suficientes de  KKT (demostración)}
		Por ser $g_i$ activa $\forall i\in \I$ en el punto $\ovx$: $g_i(\ovx)=0$. Por tanto:
		$$
		g_i(\vx)\leq g_i(\ovx),\qquad \text{para todo $\vx$ factible}.
		$$
		Por ser $g_i$, $i\in\I$ cuasi-convexas:
		\begin{equation*}
		g_i(\lambda\vx+(1-\lambda)\ovx)=g_i(\ovx+\lambda(\vx-\ovx))\leq\max\{g_i(\vx),g_i(\ovx)\}\leq g_i(\ovx)=0.
		\end{equation*}
		Por tanto, $g_i$ no crece cuando se mueve desde $\ovx$ en la dirección $\vx-\ovx$. Entonces, $(\vx-\ovx)$ es una direcci\'{o}n de descenso en $\ovx$ y:
		$$
		\gg_i(\ovx)^T(\vx-\ovx)\leq 0,\qquad\forall i\in\I.
		$$
		Como $u_i\geq 0$, multiplicando cada una de estas expresiones y sumándolas, resulta
		$$
		\sum_{i\in\I} u_i\gg(\ovx)^T(\vx-\ovx)\leq 0.
		$$
		A partir de las condiciones necesarias de K-K-T:
		$$
		\sum_{i\in\I} u_i\gg(\ovx)=-\gf(\ovx),
		$$
		Sustituyendo esta igualdad en la última desigualdad:
		$$
				\sum_{i\in\I} u_i\gg(\ovx)^T(\vx-\ovx)\leq 0\quad\Rightarrow\quad \gf(\ovx)^T(\vx-\ovx)\geq 0
		$$
		Por ser $f$ pseudo-convexa, $f(\vx)\geq f(\ovx)$,  para todo $\vx$ factible.
		
		Entonces, $\ovx$ es un mínimo global.
	
	\begin{observacion}
		Si $f$ es estrictamente pseudo-convexa, $\ovx$ es un m\'{\i}nimo \'{u}nico global.
	\end{observacion}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Condiciones suficientes de  KKT. Ejemplo}
	\begin{columns}
	\column{0.4\textwidth}{
		En el problema anterior:
		\begin{align*}
		\min\, f(x_1,x_2)= & x_1+x_2
		\\
		g_1(x_1,x_2)=& x_1^2+x_2^2-4\leq 0
		\\
		g_2(x_1,x_2)= & -x_1^2+x_2\leq 0
		\end{align*}
	}
	\column{0.6\textwidth}{
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=0.7]
			\clip(-2.2,-2.2) rectangle (3.6,2.6);    
			\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-2.2,-2.2) grid (4.2,2.6);
			\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-2.2,0.) -- (4.2,0.);
			\foreach \x in {-2,-1,1,2,3,4} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
			\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0.,-2.2) -- (0.,2.6);
			\foreach \y in {-2,-1,1,2} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
			\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
			\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.25] (-10,-10) -- (-10,10) -- (10,10) -- (10,-10) -- (-10,-10);
			\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2.2:4.2,color=yellow,fill=white,fill opacity=1,samples=100] plot(\x,{(\x*\x)});
			\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.25] (0.,0.) circle (2cm);
			\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(--3.-1.*\x)/1.});
			\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(--3.5-1.*\x)/1.});
			\draw [->,line width=1.5pt, color=red] (2.5,1.) -- +(0.75,0.75) node[above] {\tiny $\gf$};
			\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-2.2,-2.2) grid (4.2,2.6);
			\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-2.2,0.) -- (4.2,0.);
			\foreach \x in {-2,-1,1,2,3,4} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
			\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0.,-2.2) -- (0.,2.6);
			\foreach \y in {-2,-1,1,2} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
	}
\end{columns}
		\vspace*{-5mm}
		\begin{itemize}
			\item La funci\'{o}n $f$ es lineal y, por tanto, c\'{o}ncava y convexa. 
			\item Las matrices Hessiana de las restricciones son:
					$$
					\vH_1=\begin{pmatrix} 2 & 0 \\ 0 & 2 \end{pmatrix}\qquad
					\vH_2=\begin{pmatrix} -2 & 0 \\ 0 & 0  \end{pmatrix}
					$$
		\item $\vH_1$ es definida positiva: $g_1$ es  estrictamente convexa
		\item $\vH_2$ es semidefinida negativa: $g_2$  es c\'{o}ncava.
		\end{itemize}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Condiciones suficientes de  KKT. Ejemplo}
	\begin{columns}[t]
		\column[t]{0.5\textwidth}\small
				\begin{align*}
				A\,:\, &\text{ni máx ni mín} \\
				B\,:\, &\text{mín global } \\
				C\,:\, &\text{máx global} \\
				D\,:\, &\text{máx local} \\
				E\,:\, &\text{no KKT} 
				\end{align*}
		\only<2>{
			$A=(-\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{4})$
			\begin{itemize}
				\item $u_1=0$ y $u_2<0$: candidato a m\'{a}ximo.
				\item $g_2$ activa y c\'{o}ncava. 
				\item No verifica las condiciones suficientes.
			\end{itemize} 
		}
		\only<3>{
			$B=(-\sqrt{2},-\sqrt{2})$ 
			\begin{itemize}
				\item $u_1>0$ y $u_2=0$: candidato a mínimo.
				\item $g_1$ activa y convexa. 
				\item Mínimo global.
			\end{itemize} 
		}
		\only<4>{
			$C=(\sqrt{2},\sqrt{2})$
			\begin{itemize}
				\item $u_1<0$ y $u_2=0$: candidato a m\'{a}ximo.
				\item $g_1$ activa y convexa. 
				\item Máximo global.
			\end{itemize} 
		}
		\only<5>{
			$D=\bigl(\frac{-1+\sqrt{17}}{2},-\sqrt{\frac{-1+\sqrt{17}}{2}}\bigr)$ 
			\begin{itemize}
				\item $u_1<0$ y $u_2<0$: candidato a m\'{a}ximo.
				\item $g_1$ activa y convexa. 
				\item $g_2$ activa y cóncava. 
				\item No verifica las condiciones suficientes.
			\end{itemize} 
		}
		\column[t]{0.5\textwidth}
		\begin{center}
			\scalebox{0.8}{
				\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=1.5]
				\clip(-2.2,-2.2) rectangle (2.7,2.6);    
				\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1.5cm,ystep=1.5cm] (-2.2,-2.2) grid (4.2,2.6);
				\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-2.2,0.) -- (4.2,0.);
				\foreach \x in {-2,-1,1,2,3,4} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
				\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0.,-2.2) -- (0.,2.6);
				\foreach \y in {-2,-1,1,2} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
				\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
				\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.25] (-10,-10) -- (-10,10) -- (10,10) -- (10,-10) -- (-10,-10);
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2.2:4.2,color=yellow,fill=white,fill opacity=1,samples=100] plot(\x,{(\x*\x)});
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.25] (0.,0.) circle (2cm);
				%\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.-\x)});
				%\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(3.5-\x)});
				\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-1.25+1.56-\x)});
				\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(1.25+1.56-\x)});
				%\draw [->,line width=1.5pt, color=red] (2.5,1.) -- +(0.75,0.75) node[above] {$\gf$};
					\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (-1.25,1.562) -- (-1.875,2.343);
					\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (1.25,1.562) -- (1.875,2.343);
					\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-0.5,0.25) -- (0.3,1.05);
					\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (-1.25,1.562) -- (-0.25,1.962);
					\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=yellow] (1.25,1.562) -- (0.25,1.962);
					\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-0.5,0.25) -- (0.5,1.25);
					\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-1.25,1.562) -- (-0.25,2.562);
					\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (1.25,1.562) -- (2.25,2.562);
					\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) -- (-0.41421356237309515,-0.41421356237309515);
					\draw [->,line width=1pt, color=red] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) -- (2.414213562373095,2.414213562373095);
					\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) -- (2.121320343559643,2.121320343559643);
					\draw [->,line width=1.25pt,color=blue] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) -- (-2.121320343559643,-2.121320343559643);
					\draw [fill=black] (-1.4142135623730951,-1.4142135623730951) circle (1.2pt) node[above] {B};;
					\draw [fill=black] (1.4142135623730951,1.4142135623730951) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {C};
					\draw [fill=black] (1.25,1.562) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {E};;
					\draw [fill=black] (-1.25,1.562) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {D};;
					\draw [fill=black] (-0.5,0.25) circle (1.2pt) node[below] {A};

					\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-0.25-\x)});
					\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(-2.828426-\x)});
					\draw [line width=0.4pt,dash pattern=on 2pt off 2pt,domain=-2.2:4.2] plot(\x,{(2.828426-\x)});
				\end{tikzpicture}}
		\end{center}
	\end{columns}
	\visible<5>{Gráficamente se comprueba el punto D es  un  máximo local.}
\end{frame}

%\begin{frame}{Condiciones suficientes de  KKT. Ejemplo}
%		
%		Los puntos que son soluci\'{o}n de las ecuaciones de Karush-Kuhn-Tucker son:
%		\begin{enumerate}
%			\item  $A=(-\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{4})$ con multiplicadores $u_1=0$ y $u_2<0$, candidato a m\'{a}ximo, pero la \'{u}nica
%			restricci\'{o}n activa es c\'{o}ncava. No verifica las condiciones suficientes.
%			\item $B=(-\sqrt{2},-\sqrt{2})$ con $u_1>0$ y $u_2=0$. Es un candidato a m\'{\i}nimo y la \'{u}nica
%			restricci\'{o}n activa es convexa. Por tanto, es un m\'{\i}nimo global.
%			\item $C=(\sqrt{2},\sqrt{2})$ con $u_1<0$ y $u_2=0$. Es un candidato a m\'{a}ximo y la \'{u}nica
%			restricci\'{o}n activa es convexa. Por tanto, es un m\'{a}ximo global.
%			\item $D=\bigl(\frac{-1+\sqrt{17}}{2},-\sqrt{\frac{-1+\sqrt{17}}{2}}\bigr)$ con
%			$u_1<0$ y $u_2<0$. Es un candidato a m\'{a}ximo, pero una de las restricciones activas es c\'{o}ncava.
%			No verifica las condiciones suficientes, aunque gr\'{a}ficamente se puede comprobar que es
%			un m\'{a}ximo local.
%		\end{enumerate}
%\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Esquema de resoluci\'{o}n (m\'{e}todo KKT)}\footnotesize
	%%
	El problema a resolver es $\min\{f(\vx)\,/\, \vx\in\X, g_i(x)\leq 0,\; i=1,\dots,m\},
	$,	siendo $\X$ un abierto no vac\'{\i}o de $\Re^n$ y $f,g_i:\Re^n\rightarrow\Re$.
	
	Un posible esquema de resoluci\'{o}n es:
	\begin{enumerate}[(t= 1). ]
		\item Construir la funci\'{o}n 	$$
		L(\vx,\vu)=f(\vx)+\sum_{i=1}^m u_ig_i(\vx)\qquad \text{(funci\'{o}n Lagrangiana)}.
		$$
		\item Hallar los puntos K-K-T, es decir todos los puntos que verifican:
		\begin{align*}
		\frac{\delta L}{\delta x_j}=0\quad \forall j=1,\dots,n &
		\qquad\text{(condici\'{o}n de gradiente),}
		\\
		g_i(\vx)\leq 0\quad \forall i=1,\dots,m
		&\qquad\text{(condici\'{o}n de factibilidad),}
		\\
		u_ig_i(\vx)=0\quad\forall i=1\dots,m &\qquad \text{(condici\'{o}n de ortogonalidad),}
		\\
		u_i\geq 0\quad \forall i=i,\dots,m &\qquad\text{(condici\'{o}n de no negatividad).}
		\end{align*}
		\item Condici\'{o}n de independencia lineal de  $\gg_i(\vx)$, $i\in\I=\{i\,/\, g_i(\vx)=0\}$.
		\item $g_i$ cuasi-convexas diferenciables y $f$ pseudo-convexa en
		alg\'{u}n punto $\ovx$ soluci\'{o}n de las condiciones anteriores, $\ovx$ es m\'{\i}nimo global. En caso
		contrario, hay que analizar los puntos individualmente.
	\end{enumerate}
\end{frame}
\begin{frame}{Esquema de resoluci\'{o}n (m\'{e}todo KKT)}
	\begin{observacion}
		\begin{itemize}\itemsep 5mm
			\item Si el problema es de maximizaci\'{o}n, el \'{u}nico cambio en este procedimiento
			consiste en exigir $u_i\leq 0$, $\forall i=1,\dots,n$.
			\item Para identificar los puntos F-J, habr\'{\i}a que distinguir los casos en los que
			$u_0=0$ y en los que $u_0>0$. 
			\item Los puntos de F-J son aquellos en los que $u_0=0$.
		\end{itemize}
	\end{observacion}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Esquema de resoluci\'{o}n (m\'{e}todo KKT). Ejemplo}
	Aplicar las condiciones KKT para encontrar los óptimos de $f$.
	\begin{columns}[t]
		\column[t]{0.35\textwidth}\vspace*{-3mm}
				\begin{align*}
				f(x,y)= & \Bigl(x-\frac{9}{4}\Bigl)^2+\bigl(y-2\bigl)^2
				\\
				& -x+y^2 \leq 0
				\\
				& x+y\leq 6
				\\
				& x,y\geq 0
				\end{align*}
				\begin{profesor}
					\visible<2>{
					$$
					\gf=\binom{2\Bigl(x-\frac{9}{4}\Bigl)}{2(y-2)}
					$$
					$$
					\gg_1=\binom{-1}{2y}\quad
					\gg_2=\binom{1}{1}
					$$
					$$
					\gg_3=\binom{-1}{0}\quad
					\gg_4=\binom{0}{-1}
					$$
					}
				\end{profesor}
		\column[t]{0.6\textwidth}{
			\begin{center}
				\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=1]
				\clip(-.8,-.8) rectangle (6.2,4.2);    
				\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-1,-1) grid (6,6);
				\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-1.2,0.) -- (7,0);
				\foreach \x in {1,2,3,4,5,6} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
				\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0,-1) -- (0,7);
				\foreach \y in {1,2,3,4,5,6} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
				\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
				\foreach \x in {0.50,1,2} \draw [dashed, line width=0.20pt,color=black] (2.25,2) circle (\x);
				\draw [line width=1pt,color=black,fill=black] (2.25,2) circle (1.5pt);
				\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.12] (-1,-1)-- (-1,7)  -- (7,-1)  -- (-1,-1);
				\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=violet,fill opacity=0.12] (0,0)--(0,7)--(7,7)  -- (7,0) --  (0,0 );
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-4:4,color=yellow,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.12,samples=100] plot({(\x*\x)},\x);
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2:7,color=blue] plot({(6-\x)},\x);
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=violet] (8,0) -- (0,0) -- (0,8);
				\end{tikzpicture}
			\end{center}
		}
	\end{columns}
\end{frame}
\begin{profesor}
\begin{frame}[t]{Esquema de resoluci\'{o}n (m\'{e}todo KKT). Ejemplo}
	\only<1>{$$
			\binom{2\Bigl(x-\frac{9}{4}\Bigl)}{2(y-2)}+ u_1 \binom{-1}{2y} + u_2  \binom{1}{1} + u_3\binom{-1}{0}+ u_4 \binom{0}{-1} =0
	$$}
	\only<2->{\vspace*{-5mm}\small\begin{align*}
	2(x-\frac{9}{4}) -  u_1  + u_2  -  u_3&  =0 &  u_1( -x+y^2)&= 0 & u_3x&=0
	\\
	2(y-2)+ 2u_1y  + u_2  - u_4 & =0 & u_2(x+y-6)&=0 & u_4y&=0
	\\ 
	-x+y^2& \leq 0 & x+y&\leq 6 &  	-x,-y&\leq 0
	\end{align*}}
	\only<3>{\alert{$u_1= 0$, $u_2=0$, $u_3=0$, $u_4=0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=\frac{9}{4},  y=2$.
		
	Punto no factible: $-\frac{9}{4}+2^2\nleq 0$.
	
	\alert{$u_1= 0$, $u_2=0$, $u_3=0$, $u_4\neq 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow y=0$.
	$$
	\left.\begin{array}{l}
		2(x-\frac{9}{4})=0
		\\[2mm]
		-4  - u_4  =0
	\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
	\left\{\begin{array}{l}
		(x,y) = ( \frac{9}{4},0)
		\\[2mm]	
		\vu=(0,0,0,-4)
	\end{array}\right.
	$$
	Candidato a máximo.}
	\only<5>{\alert{$u_1= 0$, $u_2=0$, $u_3\neq 0$, $u_4= 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=0$.
		$$
		\left.\begin{array}{l}
		-\frac{9}{2}-u_3=0
		\\[2mm]
		2(y-2)=0 
		\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
		\left\{\begin{array}{l}
		(x,y) = (0,2)
		\\[2mm]	
		\vu=(0,0,-\frac{9}{2},0)
		\end{array}\right.
		$$
		Punto no factible: $-0+2^2\nleq 0$.}
	\only<7>{\alert{$u_1=0$, $u_2=0$, $u_3\neq 0$, $u_4\neq 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y=0$.
		$$
		\left.\begin{array}{l}
		-2\frac{9}{4}-u_3=0
		\\[2mm]
		-4  - u_4  =0
		\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
		\left\{\begin{array}{l}
		(x,y) = (0,0)
		\\[2mm]	
		\vu=(0,0,-\frac{9}{2},-4)
		\end{array}\right.
		$$
		Candidato a máximo. 
		
		Ojo, no es un punto KKT, ya que en el punto $(0,0)$ la restricción. $g_1$ también es activa y, por tanto, los gradientes no son linealmente independientes. Pero sí es un punto FJ con $u_0=1$.}
	\only<9>{\alert{$u_1=0$, $u_2\neq 0$, $u_3= 0$, $u_4=0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x+y=6$.
		$$
		\left.\begin{array}{l}
		2(x-\frac{9}{4})+u_2=0
		\\[2mm]
		2(y-2)  +u_2  =0
		\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
		\left.\begin{array}{l}
			2x-\frac{9}{2}+u_2=0
			\\[2mm]
			2(6-x-2)  +u_2  =0
		\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
		\left\{\begin{array}{l}
		(x,y) = (\frac{25}{8},\frac{23}{8})
		\\[2mm]	
		\vu=(0,\frac{-14}{8},0,0)
		\end{array}\right.
		$$
		Punto no factible: $-\frac{25}{8}+\bigl(\frac{23}{8}\bigr)^2\nleq 0$.}
	\only<10>{\alert{$u_1=0$, $u_2\neq 0$, $u_3= 0$, $u_4\neq 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow y=0, x=6$.
		$$\small
		\left.\begin{array}{l}
		2(6-\frac{9}{4})+u_2=0
		\\[2mm]
		-4  +u_2-u_4  =0
		\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
		\left.\begin{array}{l}
		\frac{15}{2}+u_2=0
		\\[2mm]
		-4+u_2  =u_4
		\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
		\left\{\begin{array}{l}
		(x,y) = (6,0)
		\\[2mm]	
		\vu=(0,\frac{-15}{2},0,\frac{-23}{2})
		\end{array}\right.
		$$
		Candidato a máximo.}
	\only<12>{\alert{$u_1= 0$, $u_2\neq 0$, $u_3\neq 0$, $u_4=0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=0, y=6$.
	
			Punto no factible: $-0+6^2\nleq 0$

	\alert{$u_1= 0$, $u_2\neq 0$, $u_3\neq 0$, $u_4\neq 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y=0, x+y=6!!!!!!$
	
			No tiene solución
   	}
	\only<13>{
		\alert{$u_1\neq 0$, $u_2=0$, $u_3=0$, $u_4=0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y^2$.
		$$
		\left.\begin{array}{l}
		2x-\frac{9}{2}-u_1=0
		\\[2mm]
		2y  - 4 +2u_1y =0
		\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
		\left\{\begin{array}{l}
		(x,y) = (2.4;1.55)
		\\[2mm]	
		\vu=(0.3;0;0;0)
		\end{array}\right.
		$$
		Candidato a mínimo}
	\only<15>{\alert{$u_1\neq 0$, $u_2= 0$, $u_3=0$, $u_4\neq 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y=0$.
	$$
	\left.\begin{array}{l}
	-2\frac{9}{4}-u_1=0
	\\[2mm]
	-4  - u_4  =0
	\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
	\left\{\begin{array}{l}
	(x,y) = (0,0)
	\\[2mm]	
	\vu=(\frac{-9}{2},0,0,-4)
	\end{array}\right.
	$$
	
	No es un punto KKT, ya que en el punto $(0,0)$ la restricciones activas no son linealmente independientes 
	
	Es un punto FJ: existen soluciones con los multiplicadores negativos  y $u_0=0$ y, por tanto, es candidato a máximo.}
	\only<16>{\alert{$u_1\neq 0$, $u_2= 0$, $u_3\neq 0$, $u_4=0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y=0$.
		$$
		\left.\begin{array}{l}
		-2\frac{9}{4}-u_1-u_3=0
		\\[2mm]
		-4   =0 !!!!!
		\end{array}\right\}
		$$
		No tiene solución, pero sabemos que no es un punto KKT, ya que en el punto $(0,0)$ la restricciones activas no son linealmente independientes 
		
		Es un punto FJ: existen soluciones con los multiplicadores negativos  y $u_0=0$ y, por tanto, es candidato a máximo.}
	\only<17>{\alert{$u_1\neq 0$, $u_2= 0$, $u_3\neq 0$, $u_4\neq 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y=0$.
	$$
	\left.\begin{array}{l}
	-2\frac{9}{4}-u_1-u_3=0
	\\[2mm]
	-4  - u_4  =0
	\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
	\left\{\begin{array}{l}
	(x,y) = (0,0)
	\\[2mm]	
	\vu=(\lambda,0,-\frac{9}{2}-\lambda,-4)\qquad \forall \lambda\in\Re
	\end{array}\right.
	$$
	
	No es un punto KKT, ya que en el punto $(0,0)$ la restricciones activas no son linealmente independientes 
	
	Es un punto FJ: existen soluciones con los multiplicadores negativos  y $u_0=0$ y, por tanto, es candidato a máximo.}
	\only<18>{\alert{$u_1\neq 0$, $u_2\neq  0$, $u_3= 0$, $u_4= 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y^2, x+y=6, y>0$.
		$$
		y^2+y-6=0\quad \Rightarrow\quad y=\frac{ -1+\sqrt{1+24}}{2}=2\quad \Rightarrow\quad x=4
		$$
	
		$$
		\left.\begin{array}{l}
		2(4-\frac{9}{4})-u_1+u_2=0
		\\[2mm]
		0  +  4u_1+ u_2  =0
		\end{array}\right\}\rightarrow
		\left\{\begin{array}{l}
		(x,y) = (4,2)
		\\[2mm]	
		\vu=(\frac{7}{10},\frac{-14}{5},0,0)
		\end{array}\right.
		$$
		No verifica las condiciones suficientes. }
		\only<20>{\alert{$u_1\neq 0$, $u_2\neq  0$, $u_3= 0$, $u_4\neq 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y=0, x+y=6!!!!!$

		No tiene solución.

		\alert{$u_1\neq 0$, $u_2\neq  0$, $u_3\neq 0$, $u_4= 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y=0, x+y=6!!!!!$

		No tiene solución.

		\alert{$u_1\neq 0$, $u_2\neq  0$, $u_3\neq 0$, $u_4\neq 0$)} \quad  $\Rightarrow x=y=0, x+y=6!!!!!$

		No tiene solución.}
		\only<4,6,8,11,14,19,21>{
		\begin{center}\vspace*{-10mm}
		\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=1]
		\only<21>{
			\clip(-1.5,-.8) rectangle (14.2,3.2);    
			\draw  (7,3.00) node[right] (a) {$(2.4;1.55)$: mín global};
			\draw  (7,2.25) node[right] (a) {$(6,0)$: máx global};
			\draw  (7,1.50) node[right] (a) {$(0,0)$: máx local};
			\draw  (7,0.75) node[right] (a) {$(2.25;0)$: ni máx ni mín};
			\draw  (7,0.00) node[right] (a) {$(4,2)$: ni máx ni mín};
		}
		\clip(-1.5,-.8) rectangle (7.1,3.2);    
		\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-1,-1) grid (8,8);
		\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-1.2,0.) -- (6,0);
		\foreach \x in {-1,1,2,3,4,5,6,7} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
		\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0,-1) -- (0,6);
		\foreach \y in {1,2,3,4,5,6} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
		\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
		\foreach \x in {0.50,1,2} \draw [dashed, line width=0.20pt,color=black] (2.25,2) circle (\x);
		\draw [line width=1pt,color=black,fill=black] (2.25,2) circle (1.5pt);
		\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.12] (-1,-1)-- (-1,7)  -- (7,-1)  -- (-1,-1);
		\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=violet,fill opacity=0.12] (0,0)--(0,8)--(8,8)  -- (8,0) --  (0,0 );
		\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-4:4,color=yellow,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.12,samples=100] plot({(\x*\x)},\x);
		\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2:8,color=blue] plot({(6-\x)},\x);
		\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=violet] (8,0) -- (0,0) -- (0,8);
		\visible<4,21>{
				\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=violet] (2.25,0) --+ (0,-0.75);
				\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (2.25,0) --+ (0,-0.5);
				\draw [line width=1pt,color=red,fill=red] (2.25,0) circle (1.5pt);
		}
		\visible<6>{
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=violet] (0,2) --+ (-0.75,0);
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (0,2) -- + (-0.5,0);
			\draw [line width=1pt,color=red,fill=red] (0,2) circle (1.5pt);
		}
		\visible<8,21>{
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (0,0) --+ (-1.125,-0.5);
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=violet] (0,0) --+ (0,-0.75);
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=violet] (0,0) --+ (-0.75,0);
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=yellow] (0,0) --+ (-0.50,0);
			\draw [line width=1pt,color=red,fill=red] (0,0) circle (1.5pt);
		}
		\visible<11,21>{
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (6,0) --+ (0.75,-0.4);
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=violet] (6,0) --+ (0,-0.75);
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=blue] (6,0) --+ (0.75,0.75);
			%\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=yellow] (0,0) --+ (-0.50,0);
			\draw [line width=1pt,color=red,fill=red] (6,0) circle (1.5pt);
		}
		\visible<14,21>{
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (2.4,1.55) --+ (0.15,-0.45);
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=yellow] (2.4,1.55) --+ (-0.15,0.45);
			\draw [line width=1pt,color=red,fill=red] (2.4,1.55) circle (1.5pt);
		}
		\visible<19,21>{
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (4,2) --+ (0.75,0);
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=yellow] (4,2) --+ (-0.1,0.8);
			\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=blue] (4,2) --+ (0.75,0.75);
			\draw [line width=1pt,color=red,fill=red] (4,2) circle (1.5pt);
		}
		\end{tikzpicture}
	\end{center}
}


\end{frame}
\end{profesor}
\begin{frame}{Esquema de resoluci\'{o}n (m\'{e}todo KKT). Ejemplo}
	Aplicar las condiciones KKT para encontrar los óptimos de $f$.
	\begin{columns}[t]
		\column[]{0.35\textwidth}\vspace*{-2mm}
				\begin{align*}
				f(x,y)=  & 3x-y
				\\
				& x^2+y^2 \leq 1
				\\
				& 4x+y\geq 1
				\end{align*}
				\begin{profesor}
						\visible<2->{\vspace*{-5mm}\small \begin{align*}
						&\gf=\binom{3)}{-1}
						\\
						&\gg_1=\binom{2x}{2y}\quad
						\gg_2=\binom{-4}{-1}
						\end{align*}}
					\visible<3>{\vspace*{-5mm}\begin{align*}
						3+2xu_1-4u_2 &  =0 
						\\
						-1+2yu_1-u_2 & =0
						\\
						u_1(x^2+y^2-1) & =0
						\\
						u_2(-4x-y+1) & =0
						\end{align*}}
				\end{profesor}
				
		\column[t]{0.6\textwidth}{
			\begin{center}
				\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=2]
				\clip(-1.5,-1.5) rectangle (1.5,1.5);    
				\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-2,-2) grid (6,6);
				\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-2.2,0.) -- (6,0);
				\foreach \x in {-1,1,2,3,4,5,6} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
				\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0,-2) -- (0,6);
				\foreach \y in {-1,1,2,3,4,5,6} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
				\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
				\foreach \x in {-4,-2,0,2,4} \draw [dashed, line width=0.20pt,color=black] (-2,-6-\x) -- (2,6-\x) ;
				\draw [dashed, line width=1pt,color=black] (2.25,2) circle (1pt);
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=red,fill=red,fill opacity=0.10] (0,0) circle (1cm);
				\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.10] (-2,9)-- (2,9)  -- (2,-7) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2:2,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.25] plot(\x,{(1-4*\x)});
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=black,->] (0.40,1.2) --+ (0.45,-.15) node[right] {$\gf(\vx)$} ;
				\end{tikzpicture}
			\end{center}
		}
	\end{columns}
\end{frame}
\begin{profesor}
\begin{frame}[t]{Esquema de resoluci\'{o}n (m\'{e}todo KKT). Ejemplo}\small
	\begin{columns}[t]
		\column[t]{0.60\textwidth}
		\begin{align*}
		3+2xu_1-4u_2 &  =0  & 	-1+2yu_1-u_2 & =0
		\\
		u_1(x^2+y^2-1) & =0  & 		u_2(-4x-y+1) & =0
		\\
		 x^2+y^2 & \leq 1 &  4x+y&\geq 1
		\end{align*}

		\only<2-5>{\visible<2-5>{\alert{$u_1=0, u_2=0$:} 3=0!!!!  y -1=0!!!!! 
			
		No tiene solución}
		
		\visible<3-5>{\alert{$u_1=0, u_2\neq 0$:} $u_2=0.75$ $u_2=-1$!!!! 
			
		No tiene solución}

		\visible<4-5>{\alert{$u_1\neq 0, u_2=0$:} $x^2+y^2=1$
		$$
		\left.
		\begin{array}{l}
		3-2xu_1  =0
		\\
		-1-2yu_1  =0
		\end{array}\right\}\Rightarrow 
		\left\{\begin{array}{l}
		(x,y)=(\frac{-3}{\sqrt{10}},\frac{1}{\sqrt{10}})
		\\
		(u_1,u_2) =(\frac{\sqrt{10}}{2},0)
		\end{array}\right\}\text{ y }
		\left\{\begin{array}{l}
		(x,y)=(\frac{3}{\sqrt{10}},\frac{-1}{\sqrt{10}})
		\\
		(u_1,u_2) =(\frac{-\sqrt{10}}{2},0)
		\end{array}\right\}
		$$
		}
		\visible<5-5>{\alert{$u_1\neq 0, u_2\neq 0$:} $x^2+y^2=1, y=1-4x$\vspace*{-2mm}
			$$
			0=x^2+(1-4x)^2-1=17x^2-8x\Rightarrow  
			\left\{\begin{array}{@{}l@{}}
			(x,y)=(0,1)
			\\
			(u_1,u_2) =(\frac{7}{8},\frac{3}{4})
			\end{array}\right\}\text{ y }
			\left\{\begin{array}{@{}l@{}}
			(x,y)=(\frac{8}{17},\frac{-15}{17})
			\\
			(u_1,u_2) = (\frac{-119}{136},\frac{37}{68})
			\end{array}\right\}
			$$
		}}
		\only<6->{
		\begin{align*}
		(0,1):\, & \min \text{ global}
		\\[2mm]
		\bigl(\frac{3}{\sqrt{10}},\frac{-1}{\sqrt{10}}\bigr):\,& \max \text{ global}
		\\[2mm]
		\visible<6>{\bigl(\frac{8}{17},\frac{-15}{17}\bigr):\, & \text{ no punto KKT}}
		\end{align*}
		}
		\column[t]{0.4\textwidth}{
			\begin{center}
				\begin{tikzpicture}[x=1cm,y=1cm,scale=1.3]
				\clip(-1.9,-1.4) rectangle (1.9,1.55);    
				\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-2,-2) grid (6,6);
				\draw[->,thick,color=black] (-2.2,0.) -- (6,0);
				\foreach \x in {-1,1,2,3,4,5,6} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\tiny $\x$};
				\draw[->,thick,color=black] (0,-2) -- (0,6);
				\foreach \y in {-1,1,2,3,4,5,6} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\tiny $\y$};
				\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\tiny $0$};
				\visible<-3>{\foreach \x in {-4,-2,0,2,4} \draw [dashed, line width=0.20pt,color=black] (-2,-6-\x) -- (2,6-\x) ;}
				\draw [dashed, line width=1pt,color=black] (2.25,2) circle (1pt);
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=red,fill=red,fill opacity=0.10] (0,0) circle (1cm);
				\draw [line width=0pt,color=white,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.10] (-2,9)-- (2,9)  -- (2,-7) ;
				\draw [line width=1.5pt,domain=-2:2,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.25] plot(\x,{(1-4*\x)});
				%\draw [line width=1.5pt,color=black,->] (0.40,1.1) --+ (0.45,-.15) node[right] {$\gf(\vx)$} ;
				\visible<4,6->{
						\draw [dashed, line width=0.30pt,color=black,domain=-2:2] plot(\x,{(3*\x-3.17)});
						\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (0.95,-0.32) --+ (0.9,-0.3);
						\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=red] (0.95,-0.32) --+ (0.7,-0.233);
						\draw [line width=1pt,color=violet,fill=violet] (0.95,-0.32) circle (1.5pt);
				}
				\visible<4>{
						\draw [dashed, line width=0.30pt,color=black,domain=-2:2] plot(\x,{(3*\x+3.17)});
						\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (-0.95,0.32) --+ (0.9,-0.3);
						\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=red] (-0.95,0.32) --+ (-0.7,0.233);
						\draw [line width=1pt,color=violet,fill=violet] (-0.95,0.32) circle (1.5pt);
				}
				\visible<5,6->{
					\draw [dashed, line width=0.30pt,color=black,domain=-2:2] plot(\x,{(3*\x+1)});
					\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (0,1) --+ (0.9,-0.3);
					\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=blue] (0,1) --+ (-0.8,-0.2);
					\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=red] (0,1) --+ (0,0.5);
					\draw [line width=1pt,color=violet,fill=violet] (0,1) circle (1.5pt);
				}
				\visible<5,6>{
					\draw [dashed, line width=0.30pt,color=black,domain=-2:2] plot(\x,{(3*\x-2.29)});
					\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=black] (8/17,-15/17) --+ (0.9,-0.3);
					\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=blue] (8/17,-15/17) --+ (-0.8,-0.2);
					\draw[->,line width=1.5,color=red] (8/17,-15/17) --+ (4/17,-7.5/17);
					\draw [line width=1pt,color=violet,fill=violet] (8/17,-15/17) circle (1.5pt);
				}
				\end{tikzpicture}
			\end{center}
		}
	\end{columns}
	\only<7>{Además, $f$ es lineal (pseudo-cóncava y pseudo-convexa), $g_1$ es estricamente convexa (cuasi-convexa) y $g_2$ es lineal (cuasi-convexa). 
	
	\smallskip	
		
		Por tanto, los dos óptimos verifican las condiciones suficientes de KKT.
	}
\end{frame}
\end{profesor}

%%
\subsection{Problemas con restricciones de igualdad y desigualdad}
%%
\begin{frame}{Problemas con restricciones de igualdad y desigualdad}

En este caso, las restricciones que definen la región factible incluyen igualdades:
\begin{equation}
	\min\{f(\vx)\,/\, \vx\in\X,\; g_i(\vx)\leq 0,\; i=1,\dots,m,\; h_i(\vx)=0, i=1\dots,\ell\},
\end{equation}
siendo $\X$ un abierto no vac\'{\i}o de $\Re^n$ y $f,g_i,h_i:\Re^n\rightarrow\Re$.

De forma natural, la condición necesaria para que un punto $\ovx$ sea mínimo local es:
$$
\F_0\cap\G_0\cap H_0=\emptyset
$$
donde $\H_0=\{\vd\,\vert\, \gh_i(\ovx)^T\vd=0,\, i=1,\dots,\ell\}$.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Optimización con restricciones}\footnotesize
	\begin{teorema}\label{t423}
		Sea el problema 
		\begin{equation}
			\min\{f(\vx)\,/\, \vx\in\X,\; g_i(\vx)\leq 0,\; i=1,\dots,m,\; h_i(\vx)=0, i=1\dots,\ell\},
		\end{equation}
$\ovx$  una soluci\'{o}n factible del problema e $\I$ el conjunto de \'{\i}ndices de las restricciones activas en $\ovx$:

Si se verifican las siguientes propiedades:
		\begin{enumerate}
			\item $f$, $g_i$, $i\in\I$, son diferenciables en $\ovx$,
			\item $g_i$, $i\notin\I$, son continuas en $\ovx$,
			\item $h_i$, $i=1,\dots,\ell$ son continuamente diferenciables en $\ovx$.
			\item $\gh_i$, $i=1,\dots,\ell$ son linealmente independientes en $\ovx$..
			\item $\ovx$ es un m\'{\i}nimo local de $f$,
		\end{enumerate}
		entonces, $\D_0\cap\G_0\cap\H_0 =\emptyset$, 	donde 
		$$	
		\G_0=\{ \vd\in\Re^n\,/\,\gg_i(\ovx)^T\vd<0,\, \forall i\in\I\}\text{ y }\H_0=\{\vd\,\vert\, \gh_i(\ovx)^T\vd=0,\text i=1,\dots,\ell\}
		$$
	\end{teorema}
	%%
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT (con igualdades y desigualdades)}\footnotesize
	\begin{teorema}[Condiciones necesarias de Karush-Kuhn-Tucker]
		Sea el problema $\min\{f(\vx)\,/\, \vx\in\X,\; g_i(\vx)\leq 0,\; i=1,\dots,m,\; h_i(\vx)=0, i=1\dots,\ell\}$,  $\ovx$ una soluci\'{o}n factible, e $\I=\{i\,/\, g_i(\ovx)=0\}$ tal que 1) $f$ $g_i$, $i\in\I$ son diferenciables en $\ovx$,  2) $g_i$, $i\notin\I$ son continuas en $\ovx$, 3) $h_i$, $i=1,\dots,\ell$ son continuamente diferenciables en $\ovx$, y 4) $\gg_i(\ovx)$, $i\in\I$ y $\gh_i$, $i=1,\dots,\ell$ son linealmente independientes  en $\ovx$.
		
		\smallskip
		
		Entonces, si $\ovx$ es un m\'{\i}nimo local de $f$,  existen escalares $\{u_i, i\in\I,v_j,j=1,\dots,\ell\}$, con $u_i\geq 0$, $\forall i\in\I$ tales que:
		$$
		\gf(\ovx)+\sum_{i\in\I} u_i\gg_i(\ovx)+\sum_{j=1}^\ell v_j\gh_j(\ovx)= \vv.
		$$
		\\
		Si adem\'{a}s se verifica que las funciones $\{g_i,\, i\notin \I\}$ son diferenciables en $\ovx$,
		las condiciones anteriores se pueden escribir de la forma
		\begin{align*}
			\gf(\ovx)+\sum_{i=1}^m u_i\gg_i(\ovx) +\sum_{j=1}^\ell v_j\gh_j (\ovx)=\vv, &
			\\
			u_ig_i(\ovx) =0, & \qquad\forall i=1,\dots,m,
			\\
			u_i\geq 0, & \qquad\forall i=1,\dots,m.
		\end{align*}
	\end{teorema}
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT (con igualdades y desigualdades)}
	
Dado el conjunto de $\Re^2$ definido por las restricciones:
	\begin{columns}
		\column{0.5\textwidth}
		\begin{align*}
			x_1^2+x_2^2-5 & \leq 0,
			\\
			-x_1 &\leq 0,
			\\
			-x_2 &\leq 0,
			\\
			x_1+x_2-3 &= 0,
		\end{align*}
	y las funciones:
	\begin{align*}
	 f_1(x_1,x_2)  & = x_1^2+x_2^2, \\
	 f_2(x_1,x_2) & = (x_1-3)^2+(x_2-2)^2
	\end{align*}
		\column{0.45\textwidth}
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tikzpicture}[line cap=round,line join=round,>=triangle 45,x=1.0cm,y=1.0cm,scale=1.3]
				\clip(-0.5,-0.5) rectangle (3.2,3.2); 
				\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-3,-3) grid (4,3);
				%\draw[->,color=black] (-2.5,0.) -- (4,0.);
				\foreach \x in {-3,-2,,-1,1,2,3,4} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\color{gray}\tiny $\x$};
				%\draw[->,color=black] (0.,-2.5) -- (0.,3.2);
				\foreach \y in {-3,-2,-1,1,2,3} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\color{gray}\tiny $\y$};
				\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\color{gray}\tiny $0$};
				%\draw[line width=0pt,color=white,fill=violet,fill opacity=0.10] (-3,3)--(0,3) --(4,-1)-- (4,-3)--(-3,-3)--(-3,3);
				\draw[line width=0pt,color=white,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.10] (0,0)--(0,3) --(4,3)-- (4,0)--(0,0);;
				\draw [rotate around={0.:(0.,0.)},line width=0.5pt,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.10] (0,0) circle (2.2361);
				\draw[line width=0.5pt,color=red](0,3)-- (4,-1);
				\draw[line width=0.5pt,color=yellow](0,4)-- (0,-3);
				\draw[line width=0.5pt,color=yellow](-3,0)-- (3,0);
				%\draw[line width=2pt,color=yellow](0,2.2361) -- (0,0)-- (2.2361,0);
				\draw[line width=1.5pt,color=red](1,2)-- (2,1);
				%\draw[line width=2pt,color=blue] (2.2361,0) arc (0:27:2.2361);
				%\draw[line width=2pt,color=blue] (0,2.2361) arc (90:63:2.2361);
				\begin{scriptsize}
					\draw [fill=black] (1,2) circle (1.5pt) node[above right] {\footnotesize $(1,2)$};
					\draw [fill=black] (2,1) circle (1.5pt) node[above right] {\footnotesize $(2,1)$};
					\draw [fill=orange,orange] (0,0) circle (1.5pt) node[below] {\tiny $(0,0)$};
					\draw [fill=orange,orange] (3,2) circle (1.5pt) node[below] {\tiny $(3,2)$};
					%\draw [fill=black] (0,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {\footnotesize $(0,0)$};
					%\draw [fill=black] (0,2.2361) circle (2pt) node[above] {\footnotesize $(0,\sqrt{5})$};
					%\draw [fill=black] (2.2361,0) circle (2pt) node[below right] {\footnotesize $(\sqrt{5},0)$};
				\end{scriptsize}
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
	\end{columns}
Obtener los puntos que verifican las condiciones necesarias de KKT para ser mínimos locales de $f_1$ y $f_2$.
\end{frame}

\begin{frame}{Condiciones KKT (con igualdades y desigualdades)}
	Dado el conjunto de $\Re^2$ definido por las restricciones:
	\begin{columns}
		\column{0.5\textwidth}
		\begin{align*}
			x_1^2+x_2^2-5 & \leq 0,
			\\
			-x_1 &\leq 0,
			\\
			-x_2 &\leq 0,
			\\
			x_1+2x_2-4 &= 0,
		\end{align*}
		y las funciones:
		\begin{align*}
			f_1(x_1,x_2)  & = x_1^2+x_2^2, \\
			f_2(x_1,x_2) & = (x_1-3)^2+(x_2-2)^2
		\end{align*}
		\column{0.45\textwidth}
		\begin{center}
			\begin{tikzpicture}[line cap=round,line join=round,>=triangle 45,x=1.0cm,y=1.0cm,scale=1.3]
				\clip(-0.5,-0.5) rectangle (3.2,3.2); 
				\draw [color=lightgray, xstep=1cm,ystep=1cm] (-3,-3) grid (5,4);
				%\draw[->,color=black] (-2.5,0.) -- (4,0.);
				\foreach \x in {-3,-2,,-1,1,2,3,4} \draw[shift={(\x,0)},color=black] (0pt,2pt) -- (0pt,-2pt) node[below] {\color{gray}\tiny $\x$};
				%\draw[->,color=black] (0.,-2.5) -- (0.,3.2);
				\foreach \y in {-3,-2,-1,1,2,3} \draw[shift={(0,\y)},color=black] (2pt,0pt) -- (-2pt,0pt) node[left] {\color{gray}\tiny $\y$};
				\draw[color=black] (0pt,-10pt) node[right] {\color{gray}\tiny $0$};
				%\draw[line width=0pt,color=white,fill=violet,fill opacity=0.10] (-1,2.5)--(5,-0.5) --(-1,-0.5)-- (-1,2.5);
				\draw[line width=0pt,color=white,fill=yellow,fill opacity=0.10] (0,0)--(0,4) --(5,4)-- (5,0)--(0,0);
				\draw [rotate around={0.:(0.,0.)},line width=0.5pt,color=blue,fill=blue,fill opacity=0.10] (0,0) circle (2.2361);
				\draw[line width=0.5pt,color=red](-1,2.5)-- (5,-0.5);
				\draw[line width=0.5pt,color=yellow](0,4)-- (0,-3);
				\draw[line width=0.5pt,color=yellow](-3,0)-- (3,0);
				%\draw[line width=2pt,color=yellow](0,2) -- (0,0)-- (2.2361,0);
				\draw[line width=2pt,color=red](0,2)-- (2,1);
				%\draw[line width=2pt,color=blue] (2.2361,0) arc (0:27:2.2361);
				%\draw[line width=2pt,color=blue] (0,2.2361) arc (90:63:2.2361);
				\begin{scriptsize}
					%\draw [fill=black] (1,2) circle (2pt) node[above right] {\footnotesize $(1,2)$};
					\draw [fill=black] (2,1) circle (2pt) node[above right] {\footnotesize $(2,1)$};
					\draw [fill=orange,orange] (0,0) circle (2pt) node[below] {\tiny $(0,0)$};
					\draw [fill=black] (0,2) circle (2pt) node[below] {\footnotesize $(0,2)$};
					%\draw [fill=black] (2.2361,0) circle (2pt) node[below right] {\footnotesize $(\sqrt{5},0)$};
					\draw [fill=orange,orange] (3,2) circle (2pt) node[below] {\tiny $(3,2)$};
					
				\end{scriptsize}
			\end{tikzpicture}
		\end{center}
	\end{columns}
	Obtener los puntos que verifican las condiciones necesarias de KKT para ser mínimos locales de $f_1$ y $f_2$.
\end{frame}



\end{document}
